ops.title: Visual QA harness for the cc-present web SPA
ops.body:
Recipe for rendering + screenshotting a live cc-present board for visual QA. Proven on the v0.9.4 dark+mobile pass.

## 1. Serve the board
- **Shipped/installed CSS:** `cc-present start --new --session <qa-name> --doc board.json` → daemon binds an ephemeral port (written to `~/.cc-present/http.json`) and prints `http://127.0.0.1:<port>/p/<ref>`. The daemon's *embedded* SPA is whatever version is installed (brew), so this shows the shipped build. `channel: inactive` is expected for an isolated session. Tear down with `cc-present close --session <qa-name>`.
- **Working-tree CSS/TSX (to verify an unshipped fix):** the daemon only serves *data*; run the working-tree SPA via vite and proxy data to the daemon: `cd web && CC_PRESENT_DEV_PORT=<port> bun run dev` → vite on `:5173` proxies `/api`,`/events`,`/assets`,`/packs` to the daemon (`web/vite.config.ts`). Screenshot `http://localhost:5173/p/<ref>`. HMR picks up CSS edits live — no Go rebuild needed. `@cc-interact/react` is a local sibling checkout (`/Users/yasyf/Code/cc-interact`), so base tokens live in `web/dist/base.css` there, not node_modules.

## 2. Headless screenshots (playwright-core + cached chromium)
- `cd /tmp/<qa> && bun add playwright-core`. Chromium is cached from prior runs at `~/Library/Caches/ms-playwright/chromium_headless_shell-*/chrome-headless-shell-mac-arm64/chrome-headless-shell` (Apple-silicon dir is `*-mac-arm64`). Launch with `chromium.launch({ executablePath: EXE, headless: true })`.
- **Never `waitUntil:'networkidle'`** — the board holds an SSE stream open forever, so it never idles and `goto` times out. Use `waitUntil:'domcontentloaded'` + `waitForSelector('.option')` (or the block class you need), then `page.evaluate(() => document.fonts.ready)`.
- **Theme:** `ctx.addInitScript((t) => localStorage.setItem('cc-present:theme', t), 'dark')` before `goto`. Values `light`|`dark`|`system` (`web/src/theme.ts`). Tone tints resolve via CSS `light-dark()` keyed on `data-theme`.
- **Board vs focus mode:** default is focus (one block per step). Click the `BOARD` toggle (`getByRole('button',{name:/^board$/i})`) to render every block in one scroll.
- **Keymap chips (`.option-index`, 1–9):** render when the choice block has keyboard focus — which is the default in headless, so you get the worst-case chip-overlap layout for free.
- **Responsive:** set `viewport.width` — folds are width-based `@media (max-width: 720px)` / `440px`. Check 1280 / 720 / 440 / 390 / 360. `isMobile/hasTouch` optional.

## 3. Beat the 20 KB image-read guard
The Read tool blocks images >20 KB (`sips` is your friend). Full-page dark shots are 30–400 KB, so:
- Compress: `sips -s format jpeg -s formatOptions <Q> --resampleWidth <W> in.png --out out.jpg` — loop Q down (55→20) and W down until `<19500` bytes. **sips can't reliably overwrite in place** — always write a distinct `-s.jpg`.
- Tall full-page shots stay over-cap even at low Q (height drives bytes). Emit **JPEG tiles** directly from playwright: `page.screenshot({ type:'jpeg', quality:40, clip:{x:0,y,width,height} })` at `deviceScaleFactor:1`, stepping `y` in ~460–500px bands.
- Zoomed detail (e.g. chip/title overlap): `locator.screenshot()` on a single `.option` at `deviceScaleFactor:3`, then resample to ~680px wide.
- Programmatic checks beat pixels for exact sizes: `await locator.boundingBox()` confirmed the 44px close-button fix without a screenshot.

## 4. What's objective vs eyeball
- **Contrast is math** — compute WCAG relative-luminance ratios for tone-on-surface pairs (dark `--card` #1e222b, tones `--ok`/`--warn`/`--danger`). The Blue-Pencil dark palette clears AA (6.1–7.4:1). Don't eyeball what you can compute.
- **iOS parity** is often a code read, not a sim: `BlockPalette` uses `Color(hexLight:hexDark:)` (adaptive `UIColor` by `userInterfaceStyle`) with hex identical to the web `light-dark()` tokens, so iOS dark contrast == web by construction. The iOS app gates boards behind machine-pairing and the daemon binds 127.0.0.1, so a live sim board isn't worth the setup for a pure render check.
ops.when: Doing a visual/design QA pass on the cc-present web SPA — dark theme, mobile/responsive widths, or block rendering — where you need to eyeball actual pixels, not just read code
